checks.description: Skill must not claim Microsoft 365 or Graph access unless a connector/tool or user-provided data is available.
Skill must recommend narrow read-only or scoped permissions by default and justify broad permissions.
Skill must require explicit confirmation before sending, deleting, sharing, permissioning, publishing, or bulk-changing Microsoft 365 resources.
Skill must minimize display of sensitive data and respect authorization, retention, DLP, sensitivity, and compliance constraints when known.
Skill must not ask users to paste secrets, refresh tokens, client secrets, private keys, or admin credentials.
Skill must distinguish delegated/user-context access from application/app-only access when discussing Microsoft Graph permissions.
Skill output must state data source, action status, assumptions, permission dependencies, caveats, and next safe action.

notes: This baseline is a non-destructive repair planning artifact. Subsequent PRs should update the skill bodies one at a time.
